name: barbell pin presses
instructions: Set up a barbell on a power rack at chest height.
Stand facing the barbell and position yourself underneath it, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grip the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest, with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell down towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Set up a barbell on a power rack at chest height. Stand facing the barbell and position yourself underneath it, with your feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest, with your arms fully extended. Lower the barbell down towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in close to your body. Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest. Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
